title: Travelodge Makes Hotel Rooms Safer After a Bad Incident
summary: Travelodge is a hotel chain that changed its rules to keep guests safer. A man named Kyran Smith attacked a woman in her hotel room in Maidenhead in 2022. He got a key card to her room from the hotel staff without her permission. The woman was hurt, and Smith went to jail for seven and a half years. Travelodge's boss, Jo Boydell, said sorry to the woman. She promised to make new rules so that hotel staff will only give out extra keys if the person staying in the room says it's okay. Travelodge also gave training to its workers and checked its safety policies. The woman felt Travelodge did not act quickly enough and offered her only a small refund at first, which she thought was not fair. Now, Travelodge wants to learn from this mistake to protect everyone better.

when someone hurts another person on purpose

the person in charge of a company, like Jo Boydell at Travelodge
instructions that tell people what they can and cannot do

options: A woman who wants to stay anonymous

background_read: Hotels are places where people stay when they are away from home. They have rooms with doors that lock to keep guests safe. Sometimes, hotel staff give keys to guests to open their rooms. In this story, a hotel gave a key to the wrong person, which led to a bad attack. Companies like Travelodge have bosses who make rules to protect everyone. When mistakes happen, they often say sorry and try to fix things so it doesn't happen again. Learning from errors helps make places safer for all.
Article_Structure: WHO: Travelodge hotel chain, its boss Jo Boydell, a victim (a woman who wants to stay anonymous), and an attacker named Kyran Smith.
WHAT: Travelodge changed its key card policy after a man used a key to attack a woman in her hotel room, and the company apologized and made new safety rules.
WHERE: The attack happened at a Travelodge hotel in Maidenhead, Berkshire, in the UK.
WHY: To prevent similar attacks by ensuring hotel staff only give extra keys with permission from guests, and to learn from mistakes to improve guest safety.

perspectives.description: Travelodge is focusing on making hotels safer by changing rules and training staff after a bad incident.
